channel.title: IBD LitMonitor - Biomarkers & Precision Medicine
channel.description: IBD literature monitoring: Biomarkers & Precision Medicine

item.title: Association between colonoscopic findings and fecal calprotectin levels in familial Mediterranean fever patients with gastrointestinal symptoms: a retrospective cohort study.
item.description: Clinical rheumatology - Familial Mediterranean fever (FMF) is an autoinflammatory disease characterized by recurrent fever and serositis. Gastrointestinal involvement and subclinical intestinal inflammation have been repo...

item.title: TIM3 signaling in effector T cells acts as an immunometabolic switch in the purine degradation pathway to suppress intestinal inflammation.
item.description: Gastroenterology - Effector T lymphocytes have been shown to play a key role in inflammatory bowel diseases (IBD). However, the molecular pathways controlling their metabolism and functional activity remain poorly de...

item.title: Zinc supplementation ameliorates colitis in mice by sensitizing pathogenic CD4+ T cells to ferroptosis.
item.description: Clinical and experimental medicine - Inflammatory Bowel Disease (IBD) is driven by the hyperactivation of pathogenic Th1 and Th17 subsets. Although clinical zinc deficiency strongly correlates with severe IBD outcomes, the precise imm...

item.title: Identification of potential biomarkers for ulcerative colitis based on multidimensional data analysis and investigation of the mechanisms of therapeutic effects.
item.description: BMC gastroenterology - Based on multidimensional data analysis, potential biomarkers for ulcerative colitis were screened, and the effects of curcumin chitosan microspheres on the expression of key targets in ulcerative ...

item.title: Fibroblast growth factors (FGF) in the pathogenesis and treatment of inflammatory bowel diseases (IBD): An overview.
item.description: The international journal of biochemistry & cell biology - Review - Fibroblast growth factor (FGF) constitutes a diverse family of signaling proteins involved in cell proliferation, differentiation, angiogenesis, lymphangiogenesis and tissue regeneration. Emerging ...

item.title: Comparative efficiency of leucine-rich alpha-2 glycoprotein and fecal calprotectin to assess endoscopic activity in Crohn's disease: a prospective observational study.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - Endoscopic healing is a key therapeutic target for Crohn's disease (CD). However, endoscopy is invasive, and simple, non-invasive biomarkers are needed to assess endoscopic healing. Although leucin...

item.title: Molecular determinants of thromboinflammatory activation in inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: Journal of molecular medicine (Berlin, Germany) - Review - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is consistently associated with an increased risk of venous thromboembolism, particularly during active disease and hospitalization, yet thrombosis in this context ...

item.title: Development and functional adaptation of intestinal macrophages across the lifespan.
item.description: Clinical & translational immunology - Review - Macrophages of the gastrointestinal system are central regulators of gut development, homeostasis and disease, yet their origin, functional diversification and life stage-specific roles remain inco...

item.title: Enteric neuroimmune interactions in health and disease.
item.description: Trends in neurosciences - Review - The intestine contains a dense intrinsic nervous system that is closely integrated with local immune cells to coordinate motility, barrier defense, and tissue repair. Recent studies, primarily in m...

item.title: Patient-matched transcriptomics of in vivo and in vitro colonic epithelium substantiate organoids as a translational model for ulcerative colitis.
item.description: Scientific reports - Ulcerative colitis (UC) is characterized by cytokine-driven inflammation and barrier disruption in the colon, making epithelial dysfunction central to disease pathology. Intestinal epithelial organ...

item.title: The dual balance of the cytokine network: key messengers of immune activation and triggers of cytokine storm.
item.description: Biomarker research - Review - The cytokine network is the central regulatory system for intercellular communication within the immune system. Its proper function depends on the dynamic balance between pro-inflammatory and anti-...

item.title: Coordinated IFN-γ/TNF Axis Drives Selective Loss of Activated Enteric Glia in Inflammatory Bowel Diseases.
item.description: Cellular and molecular gastroenterology and hepatology - Enteric glial cells (EGCs) regulate gastrointestinal homeostasis and inflammation. While activated EGCs have been shown to support epithelial and immune balance in preclinical models, their functio...

item.title: Microbial, functional, and virulence biomarkers associated with familial risk of Crohn's disease and ulcerative colitis.
item.description: Biomarker research - First-degree relatives of patients with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) carry elevated disease risk and offer a unique window into preclinical gut microbiome alterations. We investigated whether f...

item.title: Genomic Research for Abdominal Pain in Patients with Irritable Bowel Syndrome or Inflammatory Bowel Disease Before and After Mind-Body Intervention.
item.description: Journal of pain research - Abdominal pain is a hallmark symptom of both inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) and irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and substantially impairs patients' quality of life. Relaxation response-based mind-...

item.title: The dual role of CCL20 in inflammatory disease: Maintenance of host defense and immunopathological balance.
item.description: Pathology, research and practice - Review - The chemokine, CCL20, is the only ligand of CCR6 and acts as a molecular switch in immune homeostasis, affecting both protective immunity and pathological inflammation through spatiotemporal regula...

item.title: Targeting barrier integrity: Pseudoginsenoside RT2 ameliorates ulcerative colitis by driving epithelial renewal via the Wnt/β-catenin pathway.
item.description: Phytomedicine : international journal of phytotherapy and phytopharmacology - Ulcerative colitis (UC) is a chronic, relapsing inflammatory bowel disease characterized by intestinal barrier dysfunction. Pseudoginsenoside RT2, an ocotillol-type ginsenoside widely present in Pa...

item.title: Mechanism Through Which Antioxidant Polysaccharide from Tetrastigma hemsleyanum Protects Against DSS-Induced Ulcerative Colitis: Insights from Multi-Omics.
item.description: Molecules (Basel, Switzerland) - Tetrastigma hemsleyanum polysaccharide (TH-P) exhibited anti-inflammatory and intestinal protective activities, but its mechanism against ulcerative colitis (UC) remained unclear. This study used a...

item.title: Elevated TFR1 is associated with inflammatory burden and ferroptosis in ulcerative colitis.
item.description: Frontiers in medicine - Ulcerative colitis (UC) is a chronic inflammatory bowel disease characterized by mucosal inflammation, oxidative stress, and iron metabolism dysregulation. Ferroptosis, an iron-dependent form of re...

item.title: Multi-layer transcriptomic analyses identify a mucin-associated epithelial program linked to innate inflammatory injury in ulcerative colitis.
item.description: Frontiers in immunology - Epithelial barrier disruption and mucosal innate immune activation reinforce each other in ulcerative colitis (UC), yet the epithelial glycosylation programs linking mucus homeostasis to inflammato...

item.title: An orally deliverable Co3O4@MMT nanozyme platform for inflammatory bowel disease via ROS scavenging, barrier repair, and mucosal homeostasis regulation.
item.description: Materials today. Bio - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is a chronic gastrointestinal disorder characterized by a self-perpetuating pathological loop, where excessive reactive oxygen species (ROS) induce mucosal inflamma...

item.title: Butyrate-Producing Bacteria in Intestinal Disease Therapy: Potential and Challenges.
item.description: Biotechnology journal - Review - Butyrate-producing bacteria have emerged as keystone species whose metabolic activity orchestrates host-microbial homeostasis in the human gut. This review synthesizes current understanding of how ...

item.title: Global phenotypic similarity in inflammatory bowel disease: the Eastern Mediterranean region perspective.
item.description: Journal of Crohn's & colitis -

item.title: DNA From Neutrophil Extracellular Traps Restricts Group 3 Innate Lymphoid Cells Function in Intestinal Epithelial Repair via CCDC25.
item.description: FASEB journal : official publication of the Federation of American Societies for Experimental Biology - Accumulation of neutrophil extracellular traps (NETs) in ulcerative colitis (UC) is associated with impaired intestinal epithelial barrier integrity. However, little is known about how NETs affect ...

item.title: Integrated Network Pharmacology, Metabolomics, And in vivo Validation of Liangxue Dihuang Decoction In DSS-Induced Ulcerative Colitis Mice.
item.description: Journal of visualized experiments : JoVE - Ulcerative colitis (UC) is a chronic inflammatory bowel disease characterized by persistent intestinal inflammation and metabolic dysregulation. Traditional Chinese medicine (TCM) formulas are wide...
